governingCouncil.name: Pradyot Chandra Haldar
governingCouncil.about: Former Director, Intelligence Bureau (2008) and member of the National Security Advisory Board (NSAB) 2011-2015.
governingCouncil.bio: Pradyot Chandra Haldar joined Indian Police Service in 1970 and was allocated Bihar Cadre. During his career, he remained posted in Bihar, Madhya Pradesh, Delhi and the North East region. He joined the select band of Officers earmarked for the Intelligence Bureau (IB) and served the I B over three decades before retiring (2008) as the Director, Intelligence Bureau. Recipient of both Police Medal for meritorious service and President’s Police Medal for distinguished service, he has, since 2009, served on several National level committees dealing with issues relating to National Security, viz, Task Force for Security set up by the Atomic Energy Commission; Two-Member Committee to review functioning of the Intelligence organisations; the Task Force on National Security set up by the Union Government. He was a member of the National Security Advisory Board (NSAB) from 2011 to 2015 (two terms). He has vast experience of working in the Conflict resolution. He was actively involved with the peace process with the Naga groups in the nineties. He has been actively involved with conflict resolution in the North-East region for over two decades successfully bringing a number of militant organisations to the negotiating table. He was the Govt. of India Representative for Peace Talks until 31st December 2016. He is currently President, Policy Perspectives Foundation.

governingCouncil.name: Anil Prakash Bhatnagar
governingCouncil.about: IPS (Retd.), Former DGP, Punjab and Former Advisor, Justice J S Verma Commission.
governingCouncil.bio: A P Bhatnagar IPS-1968 - Punjab (Retd.), BSc. (Ewing Christin College Allahabad University. 1962) LLB (Lucknow University, 1964), M.A (Muir Hostel, Allahabad University, 1966), former Advisor, Justice J. S. Verma (former CJI) Commission of Inquiry, former ADGP Punjab State Human Rights Commission, Former DGP Punjab (Prisons). Post retirement served as Advisor to GMR Group of Industries also served for about one year before joining IPS in 1968, as Probationary, State Bank of India. Presently Treasurer, Policy Perspectives Foundation (PPF). Author of a report on Prison Reforms for Govt. of Punjab. Convener of a feu projects relating to Child Rights and Child Protection along with British Council and Foreign & Commonwealth Office, UK. Have visited Child Protection Centres being run by the UK on the invitation of the British Council. He is currently Secretary General, PPF.

governingCouncil.name: Dhirendra Singh
governingCouncil.about: Former Union Home Secretary and member of the Commission on Center State Relations.
governingCouncil.bio: Dhirendra Singh joined the Indian Administrative Service in 1968 and was in the Karnataka Cadre. He served in various capacities in the Karnataka State Government and in the Government of India and retired in 2005 after serving as the Union Home Secretary. Post retirement he was a member of the Commission on Center State Relations which rendered it's report in March 2010.

governingCouncil.name: Sanjay Sinha
governingCouncil.bio: Sanjay Sinha is an MSc (Physics) from Ranchi University. Initially, he taught Physics in Tata College, Chaibassa (Jharkhand) for two years and had a brief stint in State Bank of Indore as a Probationary Officer before joining the IPS in the 1978 batch. He was allotted to the Manipur-Tripura cadre and placed in the Tripura part of the cadre. After initial police work in the state, he joined the Intelligence Bureau on deputation where he spent a majority of his professional career. While on deputation to the Intelligence Bureau, he did a posting in the Embassy of India in Washington DC. He returned to his cadre to head the State Police and retired as the Director General of Police, Tripura. As the DGP of Tripura, he had the privilege of receiving on behalf of Tripura Police the President’s Colour, only the fourth State Police to have been selected for the honour, from the Vice President of India in January 2012 for its outstanding success in combating insurgency in the state. He is an alumnus of the National Defence College and is a recipient of both the IPM and the PPM. He is currently serving as Treasurer ,PPF

governingCouncil.name: K M Singh
governingCouncil.about: Former Director General of the CISF and a two term member of the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA).
governingCouncil.bio: KM Singh (IPS Retd) served in the Intelligence Bureau (GoI) for nearly three decades, in the Indian High Commission London as Minister, Coordination from 1996 to 2000 and as Director General, CISF from 2003 to 2005. During his tenure in IB he served in J&K for 7 years. Post retirement, he was appointed Member of National Disaster Management Authority with status of Minister of State in the Union Govt in 2005. During his 8-years tenure in NDMA he was responsible for raising the NDRF from scratch to its present shape. He is the first recipient of ‘Subhash Chandra Bose Aapda Prabandhan Purashkar’ by the Union Govt in 2020 for his contribution in the field of Disaster Management, particularly raising the NDRF. Presently, he is associated with Policy Perspectives Foundation (PPF), a Delhi based Think Tank where he has taken pioneering initiative in the field of Management of Animals in Emergencies.

governingCouncil.name: Gen ( Retd. ) N C Vij
governingCouncil.about: 21st Chief of the Army staff and Former Vice Chairman of National Disaster Management Authority.
governingCouncil.bio: Gen Vij was the 21st Chef of the Army staff from 31 December 2002 to 31 Jan 2005. He was the Director General of Military Operation (DGMO) during the very successful operations by Indian Army for eviction of Pakistan Intruders in Kargil in 1999. He was appointed Founder Vice Chairman of National Disaster Management Authority, in the rank of Cabinet Minister, for five Years from Sept 2005 – September 2010. He was Director Vivekanand International Foundation, from June 2014- Sept 2017. He is currently serving as a Member of Governing Council, PPF

governingCouncil.name: Sankar Sen
governingCouncil.about: Former Director General National Human Rights Commission and former director Nation police academy.
governingCouncil.bio: Shri Sen is a member of the PPF Governing Bodu and at present senior fellow Institute of social sciences, New Delhi. Has been Rhodes Scholar. Writes for various newspapers of the county the statesmen, Kolkata; The Tribune, Chandigarh; The Pioneer, Delhi. He is a regular invitee to prestresses seminars, professional educational institutions of the country, prisons, police academies, administrative academics to add intellectual contact to the activities on these institutions and to suggest reforms wherever needed. Shri Sen has authored a number of books on Human Rights issues as well as some issues of social relevance. He is currently serving as a Member of the Governing Council, PPF

governingCouncil.name: T C A Rangachari
governingCouncil.about: Former Ambassador to Algeria, France and Germany; former Deputy High Commissioner to Pakistan.
governingCouncil.bio: Amb (Retd) TCA Rangachari, was India's Ambassador to Algeria, France and Germany before resigning from the Indian Foreign Service in 2006. Earlier, he spent over five years in Islamabad, Pakistan, as Deputy High Commissioner. Over a 36- year long career with the Indian Foreign Service, Amb Rangachari served in a variety of posts across different continents. Starting his career in Hong Kong, he moved to Beijing in the mid-seventies during the Cultural Revolution. Thereafter, he was at India’s Permanent Mission to the United Nations in New York, working on several UN committees, including human rights and on NGOs, and represented India on the Governing Boards of the UNDP, UNFPA and UNICEF. He served in various capacities in the Ministries of External Affairs and Finance of the Government of India in New Delhi, including the East Asia division, UN division, External Publicity Division and as Chief Passport Officer of the Government of India. Mr. Rangachari holds an MA in History from Hindu College, Delhi University. He is currently serving as a Member of the Governing Council, PPF

governingCouncil.name: Vice Admiral ( Retd. ) Shekhar Sinha
governingCouncil.about: Former Chief of the Integrated Defence Staff (IDS).
governingCouncil.bio: Vice Admiral Sinha whose decorations include PVSM AVSM NM& BAR, and honorary ADC to the President, is at present Chief of the Integrated Defence Staff (IDS), an assignment he assumed in July 2011. He will move from New Delhi to Mumbai, the headquarters of the WNC, and assume the new command on August 31. Farewell parties are already on for both him and Vice Admiral Joshi. Vice Admiral Sinha is deeply interested in strategic affairs, and has been a regular at the Institute for Defence Studies and Analyses (IDSA), the country's premier think tank. Commissioned into the Executive Branch of the Indian Navy as a fighter pilot on 1 June 1974,he is the senior-most naval aviator at present.He is an alumnus of the Defence Services Staff College, College of Naval Warfare and the National Defence College. He was born 10 August 1954. In the Flag rank, Admiral Sinha has held a wide array of appointments such as Flag Officer Naval Aviation (FONA), Assistant Chief of Naval Staff Air (ACNS - Air), the Flag Officer Commanding Western Fleet (FOCWF) and Controller Personnel Services (CPS) With over 2,700 hours of flying on various types of aircraft, mostly from on board India's aircraft carriers INS Vikrant and INS Viraat on Sea Harrier planes, he has commanded two Sea Harrier Squadrons and the Goa naval air station, where naval combat jets and other aircraft are based. Adm Sinha also commanded the Indian Coast Guard ship ICGS Ranijindan during Operation Pawan, Indian Navy ships INS Saryu, INS Shakti and guided missile destroyer INS Delhi. He was the fleet operations officer of the Western Fleet during Operation Parakram in 2001-02 and later commanded the sword arm of the navy, the Western Fleet. He is currently serving as a Member of the Governing Council, PPF

governingCouncil.name: Dr. Sushil Chandra
governingCouncil.about: Renowned Management Consultant and Institution Builder having expertise in Business Policy and Strategy.
governingCouncil.bio: Dr. S. Chandra is a renowned Management Consultant and Institution Builder having expertise in Business Policy, Systems and Strategy, Organizational Restructuring and Renewal, Human Resource Management, Training and Development, Marketing, Finance and Corporate Social Responsibility. In a career spanning several decades, he has been Consultant and Advisor to leading international and national corporate organizations, premiere educational institutions and served as member of official committees of the State and Central Government. He has advised U.N bodies like I.L.O-Bangkok; ARPLA-Manila; U.N APDAC-Kuala Lumpur and several International agencies such as I.C.P.E-Yugoslavia; Commonwealth Secretariat-London; F.E.S Bonn, D.S.E Berlin, Iraq National Oil Company and Center for Economic Development, Nepal. Dr. Chandra has been Member Board of Directors of: Export-Import Bank of India; National Mineral Development Corporation; Andhra Cements Ltd; Deccan Cements Ltd; ShamkeenMultifab Ltd; Mahaveer Spinning and Weaving Mills; Tannery and Footwear Corporation of India; Hyderabad Chemicals and Fertilizers Ltd; Andhra Pradesh Heavy Machinery Manufacturing Corporation, Small Industries Extension Training Institute and Ahluwalia Contracts (India) Pvt. Ltd; Director, Administrative Staff College of India, International Management Institute (IMI) and Strategic Advisor, Ansal University. He has also been member of several Official Committees set up by the Government of India such as Bureau of Police Research and Development; Twenty-One Member Committee on Industrial Relations; Committee on Workers Participation in Management and Equity, Training of IAS Officers, Restructuring of Weak Public Sector Banks etc. Dr Chandra has been a visiting scholar to world renowned educational institutions like University of California, Berkeley; McGill University, Montreal; Manchester Business School and University of Rhode Island. Besides travelling globally on professional assignments, he has published extensively in India and abroad. He is currently serving as a Member of the Governing Council, PPF

governingCouncil.name: Prof. Sankarshan Basu
governingCouncil.about: Professor in the Finance and Accounting Area at the Indian Institute of Management Bangalore (IIMB).
governingCouncil.bio: Sankarshan Basu is a Professor in the Finance and Accounting Area at the Indian Institute of Management Bangalore (IIMB). He has been the Chairperson of the Post Graduate Programme in Public Policy and Management (between April 2017 – March 2019), the Chairperson of Career Development Services (between April 2012 – March 2015) and Chairperson of Alumni Relations (between January 2007 – September 2011) at IIMB. Sankarshan has a B.Sc. with honours in Statistics from Presidency College, Calcutta, M.Sc. in Statistics from the Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur, a Ph.D. from the London School of Economics and Political Science, UK. His primary research and teaching interests are in financial markets and quantitative finance, particularly derivatives and fixed income securities and insurance and pension issues. Prior to joining IIMB, Sankarshan has also worked with ICICI Ltd. (now ICICI Bank Ltd.), as well as been a teaching faculty at London School of Economics and Political Science and Heriot Watt University, Edinburgh, U.K. Sankarshan has also been a Visiting Professor at University of Twente, Netherlands, Gothenburg University, Sweden, ESCP, Paris, CFVG, Ho Chi Min City and Hanoi, Vietnam and Asian Institute of Technology, Bangkok. Sankarshan is active in teaching, consulting (both in India and outside India) as well as well as research. He has published a number of papers, book chapters as well as two books. He is very well travelled and has presented his works at a number of international conferences. He also serves on the boards of a couple of companies. Sankarshan also serves on the international selection panel for the selection of Fellows for the German Chancellor Fellow, a fellowship run and administered by the Office the German Chancellor. He is also a founder member of the Indian Finance Association (IFA) and continues to serve as its Secretary since the founding of IFA in 2015. He has also served in various roles within the Asia Pacific Risk and Insurance Association (APRIA) including as President for the year 2014 – 2015. He is currently serving as a Member of the Governing Council, PPF

governingCouncil.name: P K Malhotra
governingCouncil.about: Former Union Law Secretary with more than forty years experience in the field of law.
governingCouncil.bio: Mr. P.K Malhotra has more than forty years ‘experience in the field of law in various capacities out of which, he has worked in the Ministry of Law & Justice for more than 25 years as an officer of Indian Legal Service and reached the Apex level of Union Law Secretary. He was responsible for processing and finalizing many important legislations including Criminal Law (Amendment) Act, 2013, Constitution Amendments relating to National Judicial Appointment Commission, Companies Act, 2013, Representation of People’s (Amendment) Bill, Commercial Courts Act, 2015, Arbitration & Conciliation (Amendment) Act, 2015, Insolvency And Bankruptcy Code, 2016, Constitution Amendment relating to GST, Securities Law (Amendment) Act, Consumer Protection (Amendment) Bill and many other important legislation. As Secretary in the Legislative Department he was dealing with all matters relating to election laws including references received from the Election Commission of India and litigation matters relating to election laws. Mr. P. K Malhotra has held several key positions such as Secretary-Ministry of Law and Justice (December, 2012- April, 2016); Member, Law Commission of India (ExOfficio) (December, 2012-April, 2016); Presiding Officer (Offg.) and Member, Securities Appellate Tribunal, Mumbai (July, 2010-May,2013); Chairman, Adjudicating Authority, Prevention of Money Laundering- (Addl. Charge) (February,2008-April, 2009); Judicial Member, Income-tax Appellate Tribunal (September, 2003-April,2007); Arbitrator and Appellate Authority under Permanent Machinery of Arbitration of Government of India (PMA) in more than hundred Arbitration matters. He is currently serving as a Member of the Governing Council, PPF

governingCouncil.name: R P Singh
governingCouncil.about: Former Power Grid Corp chairman and managing director.
governingCouncil.bio: Dr. Singh is a member of Governing Body of the PPF. During his 37 years of service, in PGCIL Singh has been involved in fields of engineering, contract services and human resources management. He has also worked with NTPC and Tata Steel. PGCIL got listed on the bourses in October 2007. He is currently serving as a Member of the Governing Council, PPF

distinguishedFellows.name: Prof. Gautam Sen
distinguishedFellows.about: Prof. Sen is an acclaimed expert on strategic issues and national security, currently associated with several educational institutions and think tanks. Between 2017 & 2019, he authored several significant books on India's security prisms.
distinguishedFellows.bio: Presently: distinguished visiting fellow , Centre For Land Warfare Studies(CLAWS), Delhi and Editor-in-Chief CLAWS Journal ADJUNCT PROFESSOR: National Institute of Advanced Studies, Bangalore VISITING PROFESSOR, Perspective Policy Foundation (PPF), New Delhi FOUNDER MEMBER Centre For Advance Strategic Studies, Pune Formerly: Sawarkar Professor Defence & Strategic Studies(1981-2007) Head Department of Defence & Strategic Studies(1981-2001) Director, National Centre of International Security & Defence Analysis (2002-2007), at the University of Pune Member Board of Trustees & Honorary Director General, Indian Institute of Education, PUNE (2006-2011) Air Marshal Subroto Mukherjee Chair of Excellence (2018-19). Professor Sen joined McGill University, Montreal, Canada as a post graduate student for an MA degree in political science and read political philosophy. He then went on to complete a Master’s degree in IR as Peterson Fellow at the Norman Peterson School of International Affairs, Carleton University Ottawa. In 1977, Joined as Charles E Merium Fellow at the University Of Illinois at Urbana Champaign, USA, for PhD degree program in Political Science. Member of Standing Committees of the UGC, Member of High Power Committee of the UGC to evaluate the Status of the Discipline of Defence and Strategic Studies taught in Indian Universities, Member of UGC Task force on Five Year Plans and Perspectives and many other memberships of committees set up by various bodies like the Indian Council for Social Science Research etc. Prof Sen has been consulted by members of the Parliamentary Committee on Defence from time to time.

distinguishedFellows.name: Anwar Alam
distinguishedFellows.about: Distinguished Fellow focusing on International Relations, Global Governance, and Muslim Societies.
distinguishedFellows.bio: Anwar Alam is a Distinguished Fellow with Policy Perspectives Foundation, New Delhi. Earlier, he served as full Professor in the Department of International Relations, Faculty of Economics and Administrative Science, Zirve University, Gaziantep, Turkey; Professor and Director of the Centre for West Asian Studies, Jamia Millia Islamia (JMI); Assistant and Associate Professor at the Centre for West Asian Studies, School of International Studies, Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU), New Delhi; and Lecturer, Department of Political Science, Aligarh Muslim University, Aligarh. Dr. Alam has been awarded a number of long term and short term fellowship including the Indian Council of Social Science Research (I.C.S.S.R) Doctoral Fellowship (1991), Alexander Von Humboldt (AVH) Post Doctoral Fellowship (2004-2006), Germany, AVH Renewed fellowship, Germany (June 2016, May-October 2017), International Visitor Programme (Islamic) Scholarship (2002), USA, Indo-French Social Scientist Exchange Programme Fellowship (2003, 2010), France.

distinguishedFellows.name: Nazima Parveen
distinguishedFellows.about: Expert on sub-national movements, minority rights, and the politics of space and identity in South Asia.
distinguishedFellows.bio: She is offering consultation with a critical perspective on a number of politically relevant issues like inter-State disputes and sub-national movements in India. Broadly, the focus of her work has been the rights of ethnic and religious minorities, communalisation of space, and the politics of urban transformation in colonial and postcolonial South Asia. Her book, Contested Homelands: Politics of Space and Identity is scheduled to be published by the Bloomsbury Publications. Parveen completed her Ph.D. from Victoria University of Wellington, New Zealand. She has worked as an assistant professor at Jamia Millia Islamia, New Delhi.

distinguishedFellows.name: Vaishali Basu Sharma
distinguishedFellows.about: Strategic consultant contributing to research and advocacy initiatives at PPF.
distinguishedFellows.bio: Vaishali Basu Sharma serves as a Consultant with the Policy Perspectives Foundation, contributing her expertise to various research and strategic initiatives. Her work focuses on policy analysis and academic research supporting the foundation's core objectives in national security and social resilience.

distinguishedFellows.name: Anil Kumar Das
distinguishedFellows.about: Development practitioner with extensive experience in strategic planning, research, and monitoring evaluation.
distinguishedFellows.bio: Anil Kumar Das is a development practitioner and Consultant with hands-on experience in strategic planning, research, monitoring, evaluation, documentation, project management, advocacy and partnership building. He has been working in the above domains since 1999. Some of his key accomplishments include qualitative and quantitative research, design of theoretical frameworks, preparation of manuals, and management of action projects. He has authored over 60 research, monitoring and evaluation reports, academic articles and other publications till date.

associates.name: Pooja Kumari
associates.about: Leading organization and facilitation of high-level national and international conferences for PPF.
associates.bio: Pooja Kumari is working as Chief Coordinator and Research Associate with the PPF. She joined the organisation in September, 2017. She holds a Master’s degree in Political Science from Lady Shri Ram College and a Bachelor's from Maitreyi College. She has headed critical projects like 'POCSO Act Since 2012: Its Implementation, Impact and Future Pathways', and has been a member of research teams studying custodial deaths and women's empowerment legislative intents.

associates.name: Manika Malhotra
associates.about: Economics and policy analyst specializing in environment, child rights, and national security FDI perspectives.
associates.bio: Manika Malhotra Jain has over eight years of experience in economics and policy analysis. Holding a postgraduate degree in Economics from TERI School of Advanced Studies, she has developed a comprehensive understanding of both qualitative and quantitative research methodologies. She has been a lead researcher on significant studies including e-waste management and FDI inflows from a national security perspective.

associates.name: Yugank Mishra
associates.about: Historical and sociological researcher focusing on discourse of exclusion and radicalisation.
associates.bio: Yugank has been associated with PPF since 2018 as a Research Associate. Trained as a historian, he is pursuing M.Phil in History from Ambedkar University. He has experience in historical, sociological, ethnographic, and empirical research, helping understand Modern Indian History through multiple vantage points.

associates.name: Tehmeena Rizvi
associates.about: Expert on women and child rights, peace-building, and social change in conflict zones.
associates.bio: Tehmeena joined PPF in 2019. She has interned with the Ministry of Women and Child Development and served as Bureau Chief for ICN Digital Media Group. She has been a speaker at the 43rd UN Human Rights Council and was awarded the I Volunteer Award for Social Change in 2019. She is also a co-founder of the JK Policy Institute.
